title: What to know about Passiflora incarnata before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Passiflora incarnata, commonly known as maypop, is a medicinal herb used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. It has been found to have benefits in reducing stress and promoting relaxation. The herb is valued for its therapeutic properties, including sedative, anxiolytic, and antispasmodic effects. Bioactive compounds found in Passiflora incarnata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides. A popular herbal preparation is a tea made from the dried flowers and leaves of the plant.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, the ethanolic extract of Zingiber officinale rhizomes has been found to possess several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, as seen in the decrease of carrageenan-induced paw swelling in rats. Additionally, it has shown potential in lowering blood glucose levels in rabbits, and inhibiting the growth of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria. Furthermore, its anti-inflammatory properties also extend to reducing fever and inhibiting prostaglandin release, which may contribute to its analgesic and antipyretic effects.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Passiflora incarnata are treating anxiety through its flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, which possess anxiolytic properties. It also alleviates insomnia due to its sedative effects, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids like passiflorine and chrysin. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata has been used to manage epilepsy thanks to its anticonvulsant properties, which are associated with the flavonoids and alkaloids, as well as restlessness and hyperactivity in children, resulting from its sedative effects. Furthermore, it has been employed to reduce hypertension due to its vasodilatory properties, which are linked to the flavonoids and alkaloids. Its flavonoids and passiflorine also provide relief from menstrual cramps, hysterical episodes, and nervousness, while its flavonoids and alkaloids help to alleviate digestive issues.

uses_list.description: It has been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to its ability to reduce anxiety-related symptoms through its sedative and anxiolytic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. These compounds have been shown to modulate the activity of neurotransmitters, such as GABA, serotonin, and dopamine, thereby exerting a calming effect on the nervous system.

It has been used to treat insomnia because of its sedative properties, which enable individuals to fall asleep and maintain a sleep-wake cycle. The sedative effects are thought to be mediated by the flavonoids and alkaloids present in the plant, which interact with the body's GABA receptors, leading to a calming effect and promotion of relaxation.

Historically, it has been used as an anticonvulsant to treat epilepsy due to its ability to reduce the frequency and severity of seizures. The anticonvulsant properties are attributed to the flavonoids and alkaloids present in the plant, which have been shown to modulate the activity of neurotransmitters and reduce excitatory neuronal activity.

It has been used to treat restlessness due to its sedative and anxiolytic properties, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of agitation. The flavonoids and alkaloids present in the plant interact with the body's GABA receptors, leading to a calming effect and reduction of restlessness.

It has been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce cardiovascular stress. The flavonoids and alkaloids present in the plant have been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which help to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.

It has been used to treat hyperactivity due to its sedative and anxiolytic properties, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce excess energy. The flavonoids and alkaloids present in the plant interact with the body's GABA receptors, leading to a calming effect and reduction of hyperactivity.

Historically, it has been used to treat hysteria, a condition characterized by excessive emotional or psychological distress. The sedative and anxiolytic properties of the plant help to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of anxiety and distress.

It has been used to treat nervousness due to its sedative and anxiolytic properties, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of anxiety. The flavonoids and alkaloids present in the plant interact with the body's GABA receptors, leading to a calming effect and reduction of nervousness.

It has been traditionally used to treat menstrual cramps due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps. The flavonoids and alkaloids present in the plant have been shown to have a positive effect on the uterus and reduce spasms.

It has been used to treat digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with digestive issues. The flavonoids and alkaloids present in the plant have been shown to have a positive effect on the digestive system and reduce inflammation.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Passiflora incarnata is used to treat anxiety by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters like GABA. The plant's sedative properties also make it effective in managing insomnia, allowing individuals to achieve a restful night's sleep. Additionally, studies have shown its efficacy in reducing the frequency of seizures and severity of migraines, as well as alleviating symptoms of restless leg syndrome. Furthermore, Passiflora incarnata has been used to treat epilepsy by regulating neuronal excitability, and its anxiolytic effects have also been beneficial for individuals with hyperactivity. Its antispasmodic properties have also made it useful in managing menopausal symptoms, as well as chronic pain and restlessness.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Passiflora incarnata is used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, which is alleviated through its sedative properties, as well as insomnia, often caused by its ability to regulate the nervous system. The plant's calming effects are also utilized to treat restlessness and hysteria, conditions characterized by excessive emotional or mental agitation. Additionally, it is used to manage epilepsy, a neurological disorder involving recurrent seizures, as well as hypertension, a condition where blood pressure is elevated. Furthermore, Passiflora incarnata is used to alleviate menstrual cramps, a common symptom of premenstrual syndrome, and migraines, severe headaches often accompanied by sensitivity to light and sound. Its antipyretic properties also make it a treatment option for fever, and it is occasionally used to address digestive issues, such as gastrointestinal disturbances and inflammation.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Passiflora incarnata is used to alleviate anxiety by modulating the GABAergic system and reducing stress hormones. It also helps individuals with insomnia, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality by regulating the body's circadian rhythms. Furthermore, it has anticonvulsant properties that make it beneficial for treating epilepsy, particularly in children, as it reduces the frequency and severity of seizures. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata is used to manage hyperactivity in children, as it has been shown to improve attention and behavior by regulating neurotransmitters. This herb is also used to treat menstrual cramps, hypertension, digestive issues, depression, restlessness, and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Passiflora incarnata is used to treat insomnia by inducing relaxation and reducing stress. It is also employed to alleviate anxiety through its anxiolytic properties, which have been attributed to flavonoids present in the plant. Furthermore, Passiflora incarnata is used to manage epilepsy by stabilizing nerve impulses and reducing seizures, while it has also been used to treat hysteria, which is characterized by emotional instability and irrational behavior. Additionally, the plant is used to calm restlessness and hyperactivity in children, as well as to reduce fever by inducing sweating and promoting perspiration. Passiflora incarnata is also used to treat nervousness, migraine, and digestive issues, such as bloating and abdominal pain, which are often associated with stress and anxiety.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Passiflora incarnata is used to treat insomnia and anxiety, often caused by restlessness and nervousness, which can also contribute to hypertension and epilepsy. Additionally, it is used to address hyperactivity, a condition that may be associated with tension headaches and digestive issues. Furthermore, the plant is used to alleviate nausea, which can be a symptom of various health conditions, including those mentioned above. Passiflora incarnata has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system, reduce stress, and promote relaxation. Its effectiveness in treating these conditions has been documented in various homeopathic studies and clinical trials.
benefits_description: Passiflora incarnata reduces anxiety by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, thereby modulating the activity of neurons responsible for anxiety responses. This plant relieves insomnia due to its sedative properties, which are attributed to the flavonoids and alkaloids present in its leaves. Passiflora incarnata helps depression by increasing the levels of serotonin and dopamine in the brain, which are neurotransmitters that regulate mood. It improves sleep by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, which is a result of its muscle-relaxing properties. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata decreases stress, relaxes muscles, enhances moods, reduces pain, treats ADHD by increasing attention and focus, and fights inflammation by suppressing pro-inflammatory cytokines, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine.

benefits_list.description: This benefit is attributed to the flavonoids and alkaloids present in this plant, which have been shown to interact with the central nervous system to produce anxiolytic effects, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress in individuals.

The sedative properties of this plant, particularly its ability to increase the levels of neurotransmitters such as GABA, serotonin, and dopamine, help to regulate sleep patterns and relieve insomnia in individuals.

The flavonoids and alkaloids found in this plant have been shown to have antidepressant properties, increasing the levels of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which play a crucial role in regulating mood and reducing symptoms of depression.

The sedative effects of this plant, combined with its ability to regulate sleep patterns, help to improve the quality and duration of sleep in individuals, making it an effective natural remedy for insomnia and other sleep disorders.
The anxiolytic effects of this plant, coupled with its ability to regulate the body's stress response, help to decrease stress levels and promote a sense of calm and well-being in individuals.

The flavonoids and alkaloids present in this plant have been shown to have a muscle-relaxing effect, reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation, making it an effective natural remedy for muscle spasms and cramps.

The antidepressant properties of this plant, particularly its ability to increase the levels of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, help to enhance moods and reduce symptoms of depression and anxiety in individuals.

The flavonoids and alkaloids found in this plant have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing pain and inflammation in individuals, making it an effective natural remedy for a range of pain-related conditions.

The flavonoids and alkaloids present in this plant have been shown to have a regulating effect on the central nervous system, helping to improve attention and concentration in individuals with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD).

The flavonoids and alkaloids found in this plant have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in individuals, making it an effective natural remedy for a range of inflammatory conditions.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Passiflora incarnata reduces blood pressure by exerting a hypotensive effect due to its flavonoid and alkaloid compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. This reduction in blood pressure leads to improved circulation flow, as the plant's flavonoids promote vasodilation, allowing for increased blood flow and lower resistance in the circulatory system. Furthermore, Passiflora incarnata lowers cholesterol levels by inhibiting the absorption of cholesterol in the gut and increasing the excretion of bile acids, a process mediated by its antioxidant properties. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also strengthen heart muscles, increase blood flow, and relax blood vessels, while its flavonoids and phenolic acids decrease inflammation and reduce cardiac stress. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata enhances vasodilation and improves oxygen delivery due to its flavonoids and alkaloids, which also increase the flexibility of blood vessels and reduce vascular resistance.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Passiflora incarnata reduces inflammation by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its analgesic and anti-rheumatic effects. Relaxing muscles is another key benefit, attributed to the flavonoids and alkaloids present in the plant, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system. Soothing irritation is also facilitated by the antimicrobial properties of Passiflora incarnata, which inhibit the growth of pathogenic microorganisms in the gut. Easing digestion is a result of the plant's antispasmodic properties, which help to regulate the contraction and relaxation of the intestinal muscles. The combination of these properties, including boosting probiotics, curing diarrhea, preventing constipation, treating ulcers, improving gut health, and fighting bloating, ultimately results in a balanced digestive system.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Passiflora incarnata regulates hormones by modulating the activity of estrogen receptors, thus exerting a sedative and anxiolytic effect without affecting the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis. This action also balances adrenal function by reducing the production of stress hormones such as cortisol and adrenaline. The flavonoids present in Passiflora incarnata also support thyroid function by reducing the conversion of thyroxine to triiodothyronine, thereby preventing iodine deficiency. Furthermore, Passiflora incarnata stabilizes cortisol levels by inhibiting the activity of 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1, an enzyme involved in cortisol production, thereby reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of Passiflora incarnata also improve metabolism, reduce insulin resistance, increase testosterone levels, enhance ovarian function, normalize pancreas function, and boost growth by promoting the production of growth hormone.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Passiflora incarnata soothes skin irritation by exerting its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, thereby reducing the redness and discomfort associated with irritated skin. This plant also reduces acne breakouts through its antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to skin damage and inflammation. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata calms inflammatory reactions, heals wound tissues, and protects skin from damage, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which work synergistically to promote tissue repair and regeneration. Furthermore, the plant fights fungal infections and reduces skin redness, and soothes itchy skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis, through its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory actions. By repairing skin cell damage and preventing skin aging, Passiflora incarnata promotes healthy and youthful-looking skin, which is characterized by improved elasticity, firmness, and texture.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Passiflora incarnata improves lymphatic flow by its flavonoids, which increase the contractility of lymphatic vessels. The anti-inflammatory properties of Passiflora incarnata stimulate lymph nodes, reducing swelling and promoting a healthy immune response. Its antioxidant properties boost lymphatic drainage, enhancing the removal of toxins and waste from the body. Passiflora incarnata enhances lymphatic function by its alkaloids, which increase the production of lymphatic fluid and improve its circulation. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties support the lymphatic system, reducing the risk of infection and promoting a balanced immune response, which ultimately activates lymphatic circulation, increases lymphatic filtration, regulates lymphatic fluids, reduces lymphatic congestion, and promotes lymphatic health.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Passiflora incarnata relaxes muscles due to its flavonoid and alkaloid content, particularly passiflorine and harman, which have muscle-relaxing properties. This plant reduces inflammation by containing anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and glycosides, which inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes. Passiflora incarnata eases pain by activating opioid receptors in the brain, releasing endorphins, and inhibiting pain-related neurotransmitters. Decreasing stress and improving sleep are also benefits attributed to Passiflora incarnata, which contains sedative and anxiolytic compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids that modulate the central nervous system. Passiflora incarnata fights fatigue, promotes relaxation, alleviates anxiety, lessens tension, and improves mood by exerting antioxidant and anti-stress effects through its flavonoid, alkaloid, and terpenoid content.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Passiflora incarnata reduces anxiety through its flavonoids and glycosides, which have anxiolytic properties. The flavonoids present in this plant regulate stress by activating the GABA receptor, enhancing the inhibitory effects of GABA on neural activity. Passiflora incarnata promotes relaxation by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, while its sedative properties enhance sleep. The plant's sedative and anxiolytic effects also relieve insomnia by regulating the sleep-wake cycle, and its flavonoids ease tension by reducing muscle spasms. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata improves mood by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters, alleviates depression through its anxiolytic and sedative properties, reduces nervousness by regulating the activity of neurotransmitters, and calms hyperactivity by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Passiflora incarnata regulates hormones, particularly estrogen, which helps in maintaining a balance of female reproductive hormones. Improves fertility in women with polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) by addressing insulin resistance and reducing androgen levels. Enhanced ovulation is facilitated by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which protect the ovaries from damage and promote healthy follicular development. Supporting menstrual health involves reducing menstrual cramping and discomfort through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as regulating menstrual cycles. Passiflora incarnata also reduces anxiety and promotes relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome (PMS) and other reproductive health issues.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Passiflora incarnata alleviates allergies due to its antimicrobial properties, which prevent the growth of microorganisms that trigger allergic reactions. The plant relieves respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and pneumonitis, thanks to its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the lungs. Passiflora incarnata reduces inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thus fighting fungal infections that cause respiratory issues. Its antispasmodic and expectorant properties ease congestion by promoting the removal of mucus from the respiratory tract, and treat tuberculosis by inhibiting the growth of the tubercle bacillus. Passiflora incarnata cures coughs by suppressing the cough reflex, prevents pneumonitis by reducing the formation of lung lesions, heals bronchitis by promoting the repair of damaged lung tissues, and combats asthma by relaxing bronchial muscles and reducing airway constriction.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Passiflora incarnata relieves kidney stones by its diuretic and antioxidant properties, preventing further kidney damage and inflammation. Treats urinary tract infections with its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing the severity of symptoms. Prevents kidney damage by protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby preserving renal function. Reduces bladder spasms and promotes relaxation of the urinary tract muscles with its antispasmodic and sedative properties, preventing incontinence. Passiflora incarnata also heals prostate issues, stimulates bladder function, tones kidney function, cleanses the urinary system, and supports nephrons through its adaptogenic and antioxidant effects, promoting overall urinary health and function.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Passiflora incarnata are multifaceted, as it exhibits antimicrobial activity, inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms, which is attributed to its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and saponins. Its antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, while its anti-inflammatory effects reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, both of which are associated with the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. Furthermore, Passiflora incarnata has been found to possess analgesic properties, providing relief from pain, which is partly due to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. Additionally, it has sedative and antispasmodic effects, helping to calm the nervous system and alleviate muscle spasms, respectively, due to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and volatile oils. Its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, also contribute to its antihypertensive, anticonvulsant, and anxiolytic effects, providing cardiovascular protection and alleviating anxiety and seizure disorders.

properties_list.description: Passiflora incarnata has been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms such as bacteria and fungi, which can help to prevent infections.

The plant Passiflora incarnata contains antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to oxidative stress and cell damage, and may also help to protect against chronic diseases such as cancer and neurodegenerative disorders.

The flavonoids present in Passiflora incarnata have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with inflammation, and may also help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.

The plant Passiflora incarnata has been traditionally used to relieve pain, and studies have shown that it possesses analgesic properties, which can help to reduce pain perception and alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as headaches and migraines.

Passiflora incarnata has been found to possess sedative properties, which can help to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, and improve sleep quality, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for insomnia and other sleep disorders.

The plant Passiflora incarnata contains antispasmodic compounds that can help to relax smooth muscle and alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as menstrual cramps, asthma, and other spasm-related disorders.

Studies have shown that Passiflora incarnata may help to lower blood pressure in individuals with hypertension, possibly due to its ability to reduce sympathetic nervous system activity and improve cardiovascular function.

The flavonoids present in Passiflora incarnata have been found to possess anticonvulsant properties, which can help to prevent or reduce the severity of seizures associated with conditions such as epilepsy, possibly by modulating neural activity and reducing excitatory neurotransmitter release.

Passiflora incarnata has been traditionally used to alleviate anxiety and stress, and studies have shown that it possesses anxiolytic properties, which can help to reduce anxiety-related behaviors and improve mood in individuals with anxiety disorders.

constituents.description: A class of plant compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, including quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin.
Harmonine is an alkaloid found in Passiflora incarnata with sedative and antispasmodic properties.
Saponins are a type of glycosidic compound found in Passiflora incarnata, characterized by their foaming and surface-active properties, and having a bitter and astringent taste.
Glycosides, found in Passiflora incarnata, possess sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.
Phenolic acids in Passiflora incarnata are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
Fatty acids in Passiflora incarnata are saturated and unsaturated, having antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Lignans are a type of phenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and free radical scavenging properties.
Tannins are astringent, antiseptic, and antimicrobial compounds that can exert anti-inflammatory effects and bind to proteins to form a complex.
Triterpenoids are a type of compound that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties.
Sterols are a class of compounds that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Passiflora incarnata are flavonoids such as luteolin and kaempferol which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Alkaloids like passiflorine and harmane are also present, exhibiting sedative and anxiolytic effects by modulating the GABA receptor. Saponins including passifloraside and passifloric acid have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, contributing to the plant's traditional use in treating fever and infections. Glycosides like passiflorin and passifloroside have been found to exhibit sedative and antispasmodic properties, while phenolic acids including protocatechuic acid and ferulic acid have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. These diverse medicinal constituents are responsible for the plant's therapeutic effects, which include sedation, anxiolysis, and the treatment of insomnia and other anxiety-related disorders.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Passiflora incarnata for medicinal purposes are leaves, fruits, flowers, roots, and stems. The leaves contain flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The fruits are rich in vitamin C, flavonoids, and carotenoids, contributing to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The flowers contain flavonoids, like apigenin and luteolin, which may possess anti-inflammatory and sedative properties, while the roots and stems are rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, including harman and norharman, which may have antioxidant and anxiolytic effects. The primary medicinal properties of these parts are attributed to their ability to reduce anxiety and inflammation, promote sleep, and exhibit antioxidant activities.

parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, particularly isophorone, passiflorine, and vitexin, which contribute to its sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, providing relief from anxiety, insomnia, and pain, as well as protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation.

The fruits of this plant are a good source of vitamins C and E, potassium, and flavonoids like passiflorine and isophorone, which exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, offering protection against free radicals, cell damage, and infections, as well as supporting immune function and cardiovascular health.

The flowers of this plant contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides like isophorone, passiflorine, and vitexin, which display sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, providing relaxation, pain relief, and protection against oxidative stress and inflammation, as well as supporting cardiovascular health and immune function.

The roots of this plant are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, particularly isophorone and passiflorine, which contribute to its sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, offering relief from anxiety, insomnia, and pain, as well as protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation, and supporting immune function and cardiovascular health.

The stems of this plant contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides like isophorone and passiflorine, which display sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, providing relaxation, pain relief, and protection against oxidative stress and inflammation, as well as supporting cardiovascular health and immune function.

preparations.description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness by reducing stress and promoting relaxation.
preparations.overview: Passiflora incarnata tincture is a widely used herbal preparation that has been employed to treat a range of conditions including anxiety, insomnia, restlessness, epilepsy, depression, sleep disorders, migraines and hyperactivity. It's also used to alleviate pain and muscle spasms. This preparation is very common and has been used for centuries. Its effects are often reported to be sedative and calming, which is likely due to the presence of harman, harmine and other alkaloids found in the roots, leaves and stems used to make it. The process of creating this tincture is moderately difficult as it requires precise ratios of plant material to solvent and a controlled extraction method to ensure the desired potency is achieved.
recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh or dried roots, 1 part of fresh or dried leaves, and 1 part of fresh or dried stems of Passiflora incarnata and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned roots, leaves, and stems in a clean glass jar and cover them with 80-100 proof vodka or a glycerin-based solvent to create a 1:3 plant-to-solvent ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to ensure the solvent penetrates the plant material evenly.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid tincture.
Store the Passiflora incarnata tincture in a cool, dark place and take 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed, or as directed by a qualified healthcare professional.

preparations.description: Capsule made from this plant is used for the treatment of anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, as well as to relieve symptoms of attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD).
preparations.overview: Passiflora incarnata capsule is widely used to treat anxiety, insomnia, epilepsy, depression, restlessness, hyperactivity, hypertension, migraine, sleep disorder and stress. It is a common herbal preparation, used in many parts of the world for its therapeutic properties. One of its most common effects is sedation, helping individuals to relax and fall asleep. The capsule is made from the roots and leaves of the Passiflora incarnata plant, which can be difficult to cultivate and harvest, making the production process challenging.
recipe: Harvest clean Passiflora incarnata roots and leaves, then dry them in a well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried roots and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a carrier agent such as starch or cellulose in a 1:5 ratio to facilitate capsule filling.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the herbal mixture, making sure to follow the recommended dosage of 400-800 mg per day.
Label and store the filled capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight and children's reach.

preparations.description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, seizures, and restlessness.
preparations.overview: Passiflora incarnata salve is used to treat a variety of conditions, including insomnia, anxiety, pain, restlessness, stress, skin irritations, muscle spasms, inflammation, sleep disorders, and nerve pain. It's a common preparation in herbal remedies due to its widespread use. One common effect of Passiflora incarnata salve is its ability to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. This salve is typically made with the leaves, roots, and seeds of the Passiflora incarnata plant. Its preparation is moderately difficult, requiring some knowledge of herbal extraction and salve-making techniques.
recipe: Gather 1 cup of fresh Passiflora incarnata leaves and roots, and 1 tablespoon of seeds, and dry them thoroughly.
Combine the dried leaves, roots, and seeds in a 1:1:0.1 ratio, and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Infuse 1 cup of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, with the herbal powder in a double boiler, for 2-3 hours, or until the oil is dark green.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container, and discard the solids, then add 10-20 drops of vitamin E oil as a preservative.
Store the Passiflora incarnata herbal salve in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, before applying a thin layer to affected skin areas, using a 2-5% dosage.

preparations.description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat insomnia, anxiety disorders and restlessness in children, and to alleviate symptoms of menopause.
preparations.overview: Passiflora incarnata tea is widely used to treat various conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, depression, epilepsy, restlessness, hypertension, stress, digestive issues and menopause symptoms, as well as migraines. It's one of the most common herbal teas consumed globally. Its sedative effect is reported by many users, which makes it a popular choice for those seeking relaxation. The tea is made by infusing leaves, flowers and stems of the Passiflora incarnata plant in hot water. Preparation is straightforward and involves steeping the plant material in boiling water for several minutes, allowing the active compounds to dissolve and release their properties.
recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of fresh or dried leaves and flowers of Passiflora incarnata, and 1-2 inches of fresh or dried stems, for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine the leaves, flowers, and stems in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Heat 8 ounces of water to 212°F (100°C) and pour it over the herbal mixture.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth.
Drink 1-2 cups of Passiflora incarnata herbal tea per day, as needed, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

preparations.description: Suppository made from this plant is used to treat insomnia, anxiety, and restlessness due to its sedative properties and to induce relaxation.
preparations.overview: Passiflora incarnata suppository is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, epilepsy, restlessness, migraines, depression, restless leg syndrome, nausea, hyperactivity, and muscle spasms. Although rarely prescribed, this preparation is employed in some cases to alleviate these conditions. Its effect is not as well-documented as other herbal remedies, but it has been shown to reduce symptoms in some patients. The suppository is typically made from a combination of roots, stems, seeds, fruits, leaves, and flowers of the Passiflora incarnata plant, which requires specific preparation and drying processes. The complexity of preparing this herbal remedy contributes to its relatively uncommon use.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Passiflora incarnata for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are commonly used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. Capsules are another preparation that contains a concentrated extract of the plant, used to promote relaxation and reduce stress. A salve is sometimes applied topically to alleviate symptoms of menopause and menstrual cramps. Passiflora incarnata tea is traditionally consumed to aid in sleep and reduce symptoms of restlessness in individuals with ADHD. Suppositories are less common but used to treat anxiety and insomnia by releasing the active compounds directly into the bloodstream.

side_effects.side_effect_description: Prolonged use of Passiflora incarnata causes a reduction in anxiety and a subsequent sedative effect.
Passiflora incarnata-induced respiratory depression is caused by an increase in GABA levels in the brain, leading to reduced respiratory rate and depth.
Blood pressure decreases below the normal range.
Passiflora incarnata causes drowsiness due to its sedative properties and the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids.
Exhibited impaired cognitive function and disorientation
Abrupt discontinuation of Passiflora incarnata after prolonged use leads to symptoms such as anxiety, tremors, and seizures.
Chronic use of Passiflora incarnata can lead to physical dependence.
Passiflora incarnata interacts with sedatives and central nervous system depressants, increasing the risk of excessive sedation.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Passiflora incarnata are experiencing sedation, which can be caused by its central nervous system depressant properties, leading to a range of complications. Developing respiratory depression is a potentially life-threatening consequence of excessive Passiflora incarnata use, where breathing rates decrease and oxygen levels drop. Additionally, individuals may show signs of hypotension, a drop in blood pressure that can lead to dizziness, fainting, or even organ damage. Those who experience drowsiness may struggle with concentration, judgment, and coordination, increasing the risk of accidents and injuries. If present with confusion, individuals may exhibit disorientation, agitation, or even loss of consciousness, and in severe cases, withdrawal symptoms may develop, as well as signs of dependence and interaction with other medications.

precautions.precaution_description: Passiflora incarnata can interact with sedatives, antihistamines, and other medications, causing adverse effects.

Passiflora incarnata extracts should be analyzed by a qualified laboratory using high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) or gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (GC-MS) to determine harman alkaloid content and concentrations of active compounds.
Exceeding recommended doses of Passiflora incarnata extract may cause central nervous system depression, respiratory distress, and other adverse effects, including death.
Interactions with sedatives, antihistamines, anesthetics, and certain antidepressants have been reported, including increased sedative effects and serotonin syndrome.
Monitor for central nervous system depression, including drowsiness, confusion, and respiratory depression.
Passiflora incarnata contains passifloric acid, which can stimulate uterine contractions and induce abortion, making it contraindicated during pregnancy.

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Passiflora incarnata medicinally are crucial for safe use. Consult a doctor to determine the appropriate dosage and treatment plan, especially for individuals with a history of substance abuse. Determine the potency of the plant material to avoid overdose, which can lead to adverse effects such as dizziness and respiratory depression. Assess interactions with other medications, particularly benzodiazepines and sedatives, to prevent additive effects. Monitor side effects, including gastrointestinal issues and allergic reactions, and avoid pregnancy, as high doses may induce uterine contractions.
identification: Passiflora incarnata is a perennial, evergreen, and climbing vine native to North America, with a distribution that spans eastern and south-central regions, including parts of Canada and the United States, and extending into northern Mexico. It features palmate leaves with 5-7 lobes, 3-5 inches in diameter, and 1-2 inches long, with a central leaflet and two smaller lateral leaflets. The leaves are dark green, alternate, and have a leathery texture. The vine produces yellowish-green flowers, 1-2 inches in diameter, in the summer months, followed by egg-shaped, green, and 3-4 inches long fruits that ripen to purple. Passiflora incarnata typically thrives in moist, well-drained soils and full sun to partial shade conditions.
cultivation: Passiflora incarnata is a vigorous, perennial vine native to the southeastern United States, exhibiting a twining growth habit that can reach up to 8 meters in length. To thrive, it requires full sun to partial shade and a warm climate with average temperatures above 15°C, well-draining soil with a pH between 6.0 and 7.0, and consistent moisture, although it can tolerate some drought. When cultivating Passiflora incarnata, it's essential to provide a trellis or other support to accommodate its rapid growth, prune it regularly to maintain a manageable size, and avoid over-fertilizing, as this can lead to weak and spindly growth. To promote healthy growth, it's recommended to repot the plant every 2-3 years in the spring, using a slightly larger pot and fresh potting mix, and to mulch around the base to retain moisture and suppress weeds. By following these guidelines, Passiflora incarnata can be successfully grown in a variety of settings, from containers to large gardens.
history: Passiflora incarnata is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, primarily in the indigenous cultures of North America. The plant, also known as maypop or passionflower, has been employed to treat various ailments, including anxiety, insomnia, and seizures. Native American tribes used it to make a tea for relief from convulsions and to calm nervous conditions. Early European settlers adopted its use, and it was later incorporated into the United States' pharmacopeia in 1831, where it was used to treat conditions like delirium tremens and hiccups. In 1926, the plant was recognized as a sedative and antispasmodic by the US Pharmacopeia, with its dried herb being used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness.
